wp:docPr.name: pipeline_overview
wp:docPr.descr: System architecture and data flow diagram showing all 7 modules
wp:docPr.title: Alpha Core Pipeline Overview

w:r.w:t: is a full-stack quantitative research and portfolio decision platform engineered for institutional equity trading. This document specifies the complete technical blueprint, including detailed UI component specifications, user experience design, data contracts, and implementation requirements.
Enhanced component specifications with visual design state details
Wireframe diagrams for all 7 modules with scaled imagery
User psychology and interaction flow documentation
Complete API data contracts in JSON schema format
Comprehensive edge case and error handling specifications
Manual Table of Contents with bookmarked links

Alpha Core integrates seven interconnected modules spanning market data ingestion, ML inference, strategy research, portfolio construction, and execution. The system prioritizes real-time performance (<100ms latency per module), transparency through decision audit trails, and robust error handling.

The platform uses a microservices architecture with FastAPI backend, real-time WebSocket streams for market data, SQLite for local state, and integrates LLM agents for research augmentation and decision justification.
This PRD covers the complete user-facing system architecture across 7 modules, including:
Module 1: System Bus Monitor — real-time service health
Module 2: Market Feed & Features — market data and feature computation
Module 3: Model Inference & State — regime detection and direction prediction
Module 4: Alpha Lab — strategy research, DSL editor, and backtesting
Module 5: Portfolio & Risk — position construction and risk analytics
Module 6: Order Execution — multi-gate order decisioning
Module 7: Trading Audit — event logging and compliance tracking

Alpha Core operates with a defense-in-depth security model. The FastAPI backend runs behind a reverse proxy with OAuth2/JWT authentication for all endpoints. WebSocket connections authenticate via token exchange before stream initiation.
API Authentication: OAuth2 Bearer tokens validated on every HTTP request
WebSocket Security: Token exchange + session pinning per connection
Data Encryption: TLS 1.3 for all transit; AES-256 for sensitive local state
Rate Limiting: Token-bucket algorithm, 1000 requests/min per API key
Audit Logging: All data mutations logged with user ID, timestamp, payload hash
Unauthorized API access → mitigated by OAuth2 + rate limiting
Portfolio front-running via strategy leakage → mitigated by audit logging
Model inference eavesdropping → mitigated by TLS + session isolation
Order injection via WebSocket → mitigated by token validation + order signing
Alpha Core embeds four LLM agents for research augmentation and decision transparency. Each agent integrates synchronously into the module pipeline with strict latency budgets and fallback behavior.
Takes user DSL and generates candidate factors via few-shot prompting. Outputs: Python AST tree + interpretation + risk warnings.
Latency SLA: 2 sec (timeout fallback to parsing error)
Model: GPT-4 (fallback to gpt-3.5-turbo if unavailable)
Prompt template: Few-shot factor examples + style guide
Produces human-readable explanations for trading decisions by analyzing the full decision chain (regime, predictions, risk assessment, execution outcome).
Latency SLA: 1.5 sec (timeout shows raw JSON)
Context window: ~8k tokens (last 20 events in chain)
Analyzes service health timeseries to detect anomalous patterns (e.g., latency spikes, throughput cliffs). Suggests mitigation steps.
Latency SLA: 3 sec (runs async, user can ignore)
Input: Last 1h of metrics per service
Advises on portfolio rebalancing, Kelly criterion violations, and sector concentration issues based on current position state.
Latency SLA: 2.5 sec (timeout shows Kelly-based recommendation)
Input: Current positions, realized vol, VaR estimates
Alpha Core ingests market data from multiple providers with fallback logic. Each data source has a health monitor and a queueing system to tolerate provider downtime.
yfinance: 1 min OHLCV for US equities, free tier
AKShare: Chinese market data (if expanding to CN equities), via HTTP API
IB (Interactive Brokers): Real-time tick + order execution (premium)
Each incoming candle is validated against schema (OHLCV must be positive, V > 0, O <= H, O >= L, etc.). Failed candles are quarantined and logged.
Duplicate detection: CRC32 hash of (timestamp, ticker, OHLCV)
Gap detection: missing 1min candles are interpolated with NaN
Outlier detection: price moves >5 std devs trigger alert + manual review
Recent data (last 30 days) cached in SQLite with indexes on (ticker, timestamp). Older data archived to S3. Feature computation cached with 5-min TTL.

wp:docPr.name: mod1_wireframe
wp:docPr.descr: Real-time health dashboard showing 6 service status cards, latency breakdown, and alert feed
wp:docPr.title: System Bus Monitor Wireframe

w:r.w:t: The System Bus Monitor is the first view a trader opens each morning. The design prioritizes calm reassurance: green indicators, minimal red alerts, and a clear 'glance-to-diagnose' layout. Users need confidence that the system is healthy before trusting their trade signals.

The alert feed (styled like Bloomberg) gives traders real-time visibility into infrastructure issues. A critical service downtime can invalidate trading rules—the audit trail must be impeccable. This module alleviates anxiety by proving all dependencies are monitored 24/7.
System Bus Monitor contains 4 major UI components:
Layout: 6 cards in 2 rows × 3 columns, each 280px wide × 160px tall
Data source: Polling GET /api/v1/services/{service_id}/health every 2 sec
Fields consumed: status (enum: green/yellow/red), latency_p95_ms, uptime_pct_24h, last_check_at
• Normal: green dot + service name + 'Latency: 42ms' + uptime %
• Warning: yellow dot + latency highlighted in orange
• Critical: red dot + flashing border + red text
Hover: shows 24-hour mini sparkline (latency over time) in tooltip
Click: expands to full 24h timeline chart + event log for that service

Horizontal stacked bar showing end-to-end latency decomposition. Segments: yfinance fetch (blue), feature compute (green), inference (orange), portfolio calc (red), total latency >= 300ms highlighted.
Data source: GET /api/v1/pipelines/latency, polling every 5 sec
Interaction: hover over segment → tooltip showing millisecond value + % of total
Color coding: green (<50ms), yellow (50–100ms), red (>100ms) per segment
Real-time scrolling list of system alerts, color-coded by severity.
Data source: WebSocket /ws/alerts (new alert pushes append)
Alert structure: { timestamp, severity (INFO/WARN/ERROR/CRITICAL), service, message, action_url }
Visual states: INFO=blue, WARN=yellow, ERROR=red, CRITICAL=flashing red
Interaction: click alert → navigate to /audit/{event_id} for full drill-down
Virtual scroll: only render 20 visible alerts at a time
KPI cards showing tick-level throughput: tickers/sec, trades/sec, features/sec
Visual: large number + trend arrow (↑/↓) + mini 1h sparkline
Click: expand to hourly bar chart + comparison to 7-day average
Within 2 sec of status change on backend
Every 5 sec, animate segment width changes
New alert appears <500ms after WebSocket push
Display within 10 sec of metric emission
API latency >2s: show 'Loading...' skeleton + timeout message after 3s
All services down: display prominent red banner 'System Offline' + fallback to cached metrics
WebSocket disconnection: automatically reconnect with exponential backoff, show 'Alert Feed Connecting...' overlay
Stale data (no update >10s): dim card opacity + show 'Last seen 23s ago' warning text
NaN in latency values: render as '—' (em dash) + log anomaly for investigation

wp:docPr.name: mod2_wireframe
wp:docPr.descr: Ticker selector, OHLCV candlestick chart, feature heatmap, and data quality panel
wp:docPr.title: Market Feed & Features Wireframe

w:r.w:t: Traders' most fundamental task is reading market data and extracting meaning. Module 2 combines raw price action (candlestick chart) with computed features (heatmap) in one unified view. The design philosophy is minimalist: grid lines subtle, candles prominent, colors reserved for feature signals.

The feature heatmap lets traders instantly see which of the 50+ computed features are anomalous across the portfolio (z-score colored). A red cell = that feature is 2.5σ away from its mean. This answers the trader's subconscious question: 'Is anything weird about the market right now?'
Horizontal scrollable tabs for watching multiple tickers concurrently.
Data source: GET /api/v1/tickers/watchlist on load, subscribe to /ws/watchlist_changes
Visual: active tab has blue underline, inactive tabs light gray text
Interaction: click tab → load chart + heatmap for that ticker (0.5s transition)
Keyboard: arrow keys to navigate tabs, Enter to select
TradingView Lightweight Charts library, streaming OHLCV updates via WebSocket.
Data source: WebSocket /ws/ohlcv/{ticker}, emits new candles every 1 minute
Interaction: crosshair shows OHLCV + volume at cursor; latency must be <50ms on move
Scroll: pan left/right across time; zoom in/out with mouse wheel or pinch
Click candle: shows full candle detail { open, high, low, close, volume, hl2 }
Visual: bull candles = green (close > open), bear candles = red, no-change = gray
Grid of features (rows) × tickers (columns), each cell colored by feature z-score (red = extreme, green = normal, blue = opposite extreme).
Data source: GET /api/v1/features/state?tickers=AAPL,MSFT,... polling every 30 sec
Feature list: momentum (10d, 20d, 60d), volatility, mean-reversion score, correlation to SPY, sector momentum, volume anomaly, others
Color mapping: z-score < -2.5 → dark blue, -2.5 to -1 → light blue, -1 to 1 → white, 1 to 2.5 → light red, > 2.5 → dark red
Hover: tooltip shows feature name + value + z-score + interpretation
Click cell: navigate to /features/{feature_id}/{ticker} for full history
Checklist of data validation rules for the current ticker.
Data source: GET /api/v1/data/quality/{ticker}, refreshed every 60 sec
Rules: OHLCV non-negative, O <= H, O >= L, volume > 0, no duplicate timestamps, no gaps >5 min, price not NaN
Visual: pass = green checkmark, fail = red X, warning = yellow !
Click row: shows validation error detail + affected candles + suggestion to reprocess
Summary statistics for all features of the current ticker.
Columns: Feature, Value, Mean, Std, Min, Max, Updated (timestamp)
Data source: GET /api/v1/features/stats/{ticker}, refreshed every 5 min
Sortable: click column header to sort ascending/descending
Highlight: if value > 2σ from mean, cell background is light red + bold text
< 50ms from mouse move to OHLCV update
New candle appears <2s after market data reception
Sorted correctly on header click, <300ms animation
New chart loads within 1 sec, no flicker
All 7 rules execute within 100ms, show results immediately
WebSocket disconnect: show 'Chart disconnected' overlay, queue incoming ticks, auto-reconnect
No data for ticker: show 'No market data available' + suggest selecting different ticker
Feature computation timeout (>3s): show 'Computing...' spinner, fallback to cached values after 5s
Heatmap with missing features: show '—' for missing cells, log as data quality issue
Duplicate candles detected: ignore, log CRC32 hash collision warning

wp:docPr.name: mod3_wireframe
wp:docPr.descr: HMM regime bars, direction prediction grid, fusion results, and performance charts
wp:docPr.title: Model Inference & State Wireframe

w:r.w:t: Model output is the single most important signal a trader needs to make decisions. Module 3 surfaces three layers of reasoning: regime detection (What environment are we in?), per-model direction predictions (What does each model think?), and fusion output (What do we collectively believe?).

The design uses large, clear probability bars for the HMM regime. A 92% probability of 'trending-up' regime is visually obvious. The fusion result badge (Bull/Bear/Neutral) is the focal point, colored and icon-heavy, so traders can make gut-check decisions in <1 second.
4 horizontal bars representing regime probabilities: trending-up, trending-down, mean-revert, crisis.
Data source: GET /api/v1/inference/hmm/state, polling every 5 sec
Visual: bar length proportional to probability; current regime has thick blue border
Color mapping: trending-up = green, trending-down = red, mean-revert = blue, crisis = dark red/black
Hover: tooltip shows regime name + probability % + interpretation (e.g., 'Regime: Trending-Up (92%). Market is in a strong uptrend with positive momentum.')
Animation: bars smoothly transition when probabilities change
Table: rows = 3 model types (LSTM, XGBoost, Ensemble), columns = tickers in watchlist.
Cell values: Bull (+1), Neutral (0), Bear (-1), with probability (e.g., 'Bull 87%')
Color mapping: Bull = green, Neutral = gray, Bear = red, intensity = confidence level
Click cell: opens panel with model explainability (feature importance + decision boundary)
Sort: click column header to sort by confidence descending (most confident predictions first)
For each ticker, a card showing the ensemble fusion output: Bull/Neutral/Bear badge + confidence % + all 3 model votes.
Data source: GET /api/v1/inference/fusion, polling every 10 sec
Visual: large badge with icon (↑ for Bull, = for Neutral, ↓ for Bear) + background color matching prediction
Confidence: displayed as % + circular progress ring (100% fills a full circle)
Click card: expands to show voting breakdown (e.g., 2 models Bull, 1 Neutral) + confidence score
Line chart showing prediction accuracy (% correct calls) over the last 30 days.
Data source: GET /api/v1/inference/accuracy/rolling, refreshed every 1 hour
Definition: accuracy = # correct direction calls / total direction calls (over 1-day windows)
Visual: line chart with 3 series (LSTM accuracy, XGBoost, Ensemble) + shaded region showing benchmark (buy-and-hold)
Hover: shows date + accuracy % for each model + benchmark

Visualization of Expected Calibration Error (ECE): x-axis = model confidence buckets (0-10%, 10-20%, ..., 90-100%), y-axis = observed accuracy in that bucket. Perfect calibration is a 45° diagonal line.
Data source: GET /api/v1/inference/calibration, refreshed every 1 hour
Interpretation: if model says 80% confident but only 60% correct, model is overconfident; ECE > 0.05 triggers warning badge
Visual: scatter plot + ideal diagonal line (light gray) + actual calibration curve (blue)
Click card, panel slides out in <400ms
All 3 votes visible in fusion card expansion
Model inference timeout (>2s): show 'Computing...' spinner, display last valid prediction + age (e.g., '5 min old')
Regime probability NaN or sum ≠ 1.0: log data quality issue, display warning banner
Prediction disagreement >2 models: highlight in bold, add tooltip 'Ensemble is uncertain about this call'
Accuracy < 40%: display warning badge 'Model underperforming', suggest review
ECE > 0.1: show warning badge 'Calibration Poor' + recommend retraining

wp:docPr.name: mod4_wireframe
wp:docPr.descr: Factor DSL editor, parse status, backtest results, and LLM research visualization
wp:docPr.title: Alpha Lab Wireframe

w:r.w:t: The Alpha Lab is where researchers experiment with new factors and strategies. The design bridges the gap between code and intuition: traders write DSL in a familiar editor, receive instant parse feedback, run backtests in <5 seconds, and see results in charts that reveal alpha decay, turnover costs, and regime dependency.

The LLM research loop visualization is key—it shows researchers the generative process (Factor idea → Generate candidates → Evaluate → Refine) and builds confidence in suggestions. Researchers trust suggestions more when they understand the reasoning chain.
Monaco Editor instance with custom syntax highlighting for Alpha Core DSL.
Language: Python-like DSL with keywords [def, momentum, zscore, crossover, regime, if, return]
Features: syntax highlighting, line numbers, auto-indent, bracket matching, Ctrl+Enter = run
def strategy(): m10 = momentum(close, 10) m20 = momentum(close, 20) signal = zscore(m10 - m20) return signal > 0.5
Real-time DSL parser feedback. As user types, code is validated against AST grammar.
Data source: POST /api/v1/dsl/parse with debounce (500ms after last keystroke)
Visual states: green checkmark = valid, red X = parse error with line number + message
Errors: displayed inline (red squiggly underline) + detailed error panel below editor
Performance: parser completes in <100ms, no lag perceived by user
Displays key backtest metrics after job completion.
Metrics shown: IC (Information Coefficient), ICIR, Sharpe ratio, Max DD, Turnover %, Alpha Decay
Data source: GET /api/v1/backtest/{job_id}/results after async job finishes
Job status: queued → computing → done, with progress bar + ETA
Visual: metric cards in grid, color-code by quality (green = good, red = poor)
Click metric: expands to show time-series (daily IC, daily Sharpe, etc.)
Line chart showing cumulative strategy P&L vs benchmark (SPY buy-and-hold).
Data source: GET /api/v1/backtest/{job_id}/pnl, returns daily returns array
Visual: 2 lines (strategy in blue, benchmark in gray), shaded region for outperformance
Hover: tooltip shows date + strategy return + benchmark return + daily excess
Time period: configurable via date picker (default: last 1 year of backtest)
Animated 4-step workflow showing the LLM agent's research process in real-time.
Steps: (1) Factor Idea → (2) Generate Candidates → (3) Evaluate → (4) Refine
Data source: WebSocket /ws/research/{job_id}/progress, emits updates as agent steps complete
Visual: 4-box diagram with arrows; current step is highlighted blue, completed steps are green, future steps are gray
Interaction: click step → show LLM prompt + output transcript in side panel
Animation: each step completion triggers a checkmark animation + +500ms duration
< 5 sec for 1-year backtest on SPY + 10 features
P&L chart appears within 1 sec of job completion
Updates in real-time as agent progresses
No lag typing code, Monaco renders smoothly at 60fps
Parse error: show red squiggly line + detailed error tooltip + suggestion to fix
Backtest timeout (>10s): cancel job + show 'Backtest timed out' message + suggest simpler strategy
Backtest data gap: show warning 'Backtest period has missing market data' + affected date range
LLM research loop timeout: show 'Research incomplete' + display partial results with warning badge
NaN in metrics (e.g., division by zero): display '—' (em dash) + log error for investigation

wp:docPr.name: mod5_wireframe
wp:docPr.descr: Portfolio treemap, risk gauge dashboard, Kelly sizing, and rebalance schedule
wp:docPr.title: Portfolio & Risk Engine Wireframe

w:r.w:t: Portfolio construction is the art of turning predictions into positions. Module 5 visualizes portfolio composition (treemap) and quantifies risk (gauges), giving portfolio managers instant conviction about their allocation. The design answers two questions: (1) Where is my capital deployed? (2) Am I within risk limits?

The Kelly criterion sizing panel is for power users who want mathematical optimization guidance. The rebalance schedule shows upcoming portfolio updates, reducing surprise for traders who need to execute hedges.
Enter edge %, Kelly calc shows position size
Hover → date + max DD + recovery time
Rectangular treemap visualization where each tile represents a position. Tile size = weight %, color = P&L (green for positive, red for negative).
Data source: GET /api/v1/portfolio/positions, polling every 10 sec
Tile data: ticker, weight %, market value, unrealized P&L, P&L %, entry price, current price
Visual: size α weight, color intensity α P&L % (darker = more extreme)
Interaction: hover → tooltip with position details; click → drill to position detail panel
Responsive: on mobile, convert to vertical list (treemap hard to interact with on touch)
6 radial gauge charts showing key risk metrics: VaR (95%), CVaR, Volatility, Max DD, Sharpe, Herfindahl-Hirschman Index (HHI concentration).
Data source: GET /api/v1/portfolio/risk, polling every 60 sec
Gauge ranges: VaR (0 to -10%), CVaR (0 to -15%), Vol (0 to 50%), Max DD (0 to -50%), Sharpe (-2 to +3), HHI (0 to 1)
Color zones: green (acceptable), yellow (warning), red (critical). Thresholds configured per risk manager.
Hover: shows definition of metric + interpretation + what to do if gauge is in red
Input form for power users: enter an estimated edge (win rate %) and the Kelly formula computes recommended position size.
Data source: POST /api/v1/portfolio/kelly with { edge_win_pct, odds }
Formula: f* = (p * odds - q) / odds, where p=win %, q=loss %, odds=payoff ratio
Output: recommended position size % of capital, with 1/4 Kelly (fractional Kelly) toggle
Visual: input field + 'Compute' button + result card showing f* % + warning if f* > 25% of capital
Area chart showing portfolio drawdown over last 30 days.
Data source: GET /api/v1/portfolio/drawdown_history, returns daily equity curve + max DD at each date
Visual: area under 0% filled in red (drawdown), area above 0% filled in green (recovery)
Hover: shows date + current DD + max DD in period + recovery % since trough
Zoom: select date range to drill down into specific drawdown event
Status cards showing portfolio's usage of risk limits.
Data source: GET /api/v1/portfolio/limits, returns limit definitions + current usage
Example limits: max sector exposure (e.g., Tech < 40%), max single position (< 10%), max leverage (< 1.5x)
Visual: card per limit with label + progress bar (green if <80%, yellow if 80-95%, red if >95%)
Click: shows limit configuration + option to adjust threshold (requires PM approval)
All positions render within 1 sec, no flickering on update
Update within 60 sec of portfolio change
Accurate equity curve reconstruction from returns
Red warning shows immediately when limit breached
Portfolio empty: show 'No positions' message + prompt to initiate first trade
Risk metric computation timeout: show 'Computing risk metrics...' spinner, fallback to cached value + age warning
Drawdown data gap: interpolate missing dates with NaN, show warning 'Incomplete history'
Kelly f* > 100% (negative edge): highlight in red, show tooltip 'Kelly suggests no position (negative edge detected)'
HHI > 0.3 (high concentration): show warning badge 'Portfolio is concentrated', suggest rebalance

wp:docPr.name: mod6_wireframe
wp:docPr.descr: Multi-gate score visualization, order queue, execution analytics, and decision chain
wp:docPr.title: Order Execution Gateway Wireframe

w:r.w:t: Execution is where strategy becomes reality. Module 6 implements a multi-gate framework: before an order can execute, it must pass regime-based gates, risk gates, and market microstructure gates. The design visualizes all three gates and their scores, so traders understand WHY an order is held, pending, or executed.

The decision chain (signal → regime check → risk check → market check → execute/reject) is transparent. When an order is held, the UI shows exactly which gate is blocking it, reducing trader anxiety and building confidence in the system.
Click row → order detail + audit trail
Click step → show timestamp + decision detail
3 horizontal gauge bars representing the 3 execution gates: (1) Regime Gate, (2) Risk Gate, (3) Microstructure Gate.
Data source: GET /api/v1/execution/gates, polling every 2 sec
Each gate has a score (0-100%), pass threshold (e.g., 60%), and current status (green/yellow/red)
Regime Gate: checks if current market regime permits entry direction (e.g., no shorting in trending-up regime)
Risk Gate: checks if order would breach portfolio risk limits (VaR, concentration, leverage)
Microstructure Gate: checks if market depth + spreads are favorable for execution (avoid slippage)
Visual: bar length = score %, threshold line marked, color green if score > threshold, red otherwise
Large radial dial showing overall execution readiness (0-100%). Threshold marked at 70% (go/no-go point).
Data source: GET /api/v1/execution/composite_score (weighted avg of 3 gates)
Visual: needle points to composite score, green zone (>70%) = ready, yellow (50-70%) = caution, red (<50%) = blocked
Needle animation: smoothly transitions when score changes
Pending and executed orders in paper trading mode, showing detailed execution state.
Data source: GET /api/v1/execution/orders (live table), subscribe to /ws/orders for real-time updates
Columns: Ticker, Side (Buy/Sell), Quantity, Limit Price, Order Status, Filled %, Fill Price, Latency (ms), Created, Executed
Status visual: Pending (yellow) → Partial (blue) → Filled (green) → Rejected (red)
Click row: expands to show order detail + full execution audit trail (timestamps + gate decisions)
Sortable: click column header to sort
KPI cards and charts summarizing execution quality.
Metrics: Fill Rate (% of attempted orders filled), Avg Slippage (bps), Avg Latency (ms), Cost (% of AUM), Win Rate (%)
Data source: GET /api/v1/execution/analytics, refreshed every 1 hour
Visual: metric cards in grid with trend arrow (↑/↓) + mini sparkline (last 30 days)
Click card: expands to hourly/daily bar chart for that metric
Horizontal timeline showing the journey of an order from signal generation to fill.
Steps: Signal Generated → Regime Check → Risk Check → Microstructure Check → Order Sent → Ack → Partial Fill → Full Fill
Data source: GET /api/v1/execution/{order_id}/chain, returns array of decision steps with timestamps
Visual: boxes connected by arrows, current step highlighted blue, completed steps green, future steps gray
Click step: shows timestamp + decision detail (e.g., 'Regime Check: Current regime is Trending-Up; order is long entry = PASS')
Weighted average of 3 gates, computed in <100ms
New orders appear <500ms after WebSocket push
Matches exchange fill price within 1 basis point
All steps logged with microsecond precision
Fill rate, slippage match manual calculation
Gate score timeout: show 'Computing gate scores...' spinner, fallback to last valid score + age warning
Order rejection: show prominent red banner with rejection reason + action (e.g., 'Risk Gate failed: VaR limit breached')
Slippage spike >100bps: show warning badge 'High slippage', pause automated execution pending manual review
Decision chain gap (missing step): log data quality issue + show 'Incomplete audit trail' warning
Fill price NaN: treat as data error, refetch from exchange, log incident

wp:docPr.name: mod7_wireframe
wp:docPr.descr: Event stream, decision chain explorer, LLM decision detail, and JSON viewer
wp:docPr.title: Trading Audit Log Wireframe

w:r.w:t: Regulatory compliance and post-trade analysis require an immutable audit trail. Module 7 provides traders and compliance teams with complete visibility into every decision: what happened, when, why, and who decided. The event stream is the source of truth; the decision chain explorer provides context.

The LLM decision detail is crucial for researchers who want to understand model reasoning. A narrative explanation (generated by LLM Agent 2) makes audit trails human-readable, not just machine-readable.
Real-time scrollable list of audit events (orders, fills, risk updates, model changes, user actions).
Event types: OrderCreated, OrderFilled, OrderRejected, ModelUpdated, StrategyDeployed, RiskAlert, etc.
Columns: Timestamp, EventType, Ticker, Side, Qty, Price, Status, Detail (truncated)
Filters: date range, event type, ticker, severity (for alerts), user
Search: full-text search across event fields (ticket number, user comment, etc.)
Virtual scroll: render only visible rows (e.g., 20 at a time) for performance
Horizontal timeline showing the causal sequence of decisions leading up to a key event (e.g., order fill).
Data source: GET /api/v1/audit/events/{event_id}/chain, returns array of decisions in order
Example chain: Signal Generated (T=0) → Regime Check Pass (T=10ms) → Risk Check Pass (T=20ms) → Order Sent (T=100ms) → Order Ack (T=150ms) → Partial Fill (T=2000ms) → Full Fill (T=5000ms)
Visual: boxes (decision points) connected by arrows (causality), current event highlighted
Click box: shows timestamp + decision detail in detail panel
Human-readable explanation of a trading decision, generated by LLM Agent 2.
Content: Markdown-formatted explanation summarizing the decision chain, regime state, risk assessment, and execution outcome

Example: 'Order to buy 500 AAPL was executed successfully on 2026-04-12 at 14:30:15. Market regime was Trending-Up (92% probability), which favors long entry. VaR impact was +0.02%, within limits. Fill at $178.45 with 1bp slippage.'
Visual: clean typography, emphasis on key facts (bold), action items (colored boxes)
Checklist of compliance rules and their status. Useful for compliance teams and risk managers.
Rules: All trades logged with timestamps, no unauthorized order modifications, position limits enforced, risk limits enforced, daily P&L reconciled, etc.
Data source: GET /api/v1/audit/compliance, returns status of each rule (PASS/FAIL/WARNING)
Visual: checkbox per rule, green (pass), yellow (warning), red (fail), with detail button
Click detail: shows violations, corrective action taken, sign-off by compliance officer
Syntax-highlighted JSON viewer for the raw event structure. For advanced users / compliance audits.
Features: syntax highlighting, collapsible objects/arrays, line numbers, copy-to-clipboard
Readonly: no editing (audit trail must be immutable)
New events appear <500ms after WebSocket push
All events in chain logged with correct causality
Narrative is readable, factually accurate, concise
All rules evaluated, status matches audit trail
Event stream lag: show 'Events loading...' indicator, queue incoming events, catch up on reconnect
Decision chain gap (missing causal event): show warning 'Incomplete audit trail' + alert compliance
LLM explanation timeout: show 'Generating explanation...' spinner, fallback to raw JSON after 3s
Compliance rule failure: highlight in red + show detail panel with corrective action required
JSON viewer on mobile: switch to collapsed tree view (full JSON too verbose for small screens)
Alpha Core frontend is built on React 18 + Next.js 14, with component library following design system principles. All 7 modules are implemented as standalone pages with shared UI component library.
Efficient server state sync + real-time updates
Graceful fallback to polling, reconnection logic
Fast, modern, aligned with React best practices

Next.js static export for frontends (single-page apps), deployed to CDN with 24-hour cache headers. API routes (optional) use serverless functions for light auth logic. Build time <2 minutes, deployments <5 minutes.
Core modules 1–3 (System Bus, Market Feed, Model Inference):
Backend: FastAPI server, microservices for health checks, market data ingestion, model inference
Frontend: Modules 1, 2, 3 (basic versions, no LLM integration yet)
Data: Mock data for backtesting, connection to yfinance for live tickers
Deliverable: Working demo of real-time monitoring + market data visualization + model predictions
Factor DSL parser and backtest engine
LLM agent integration for factor generation (Agent 1) and research summary (Agent 2)
Risk engine: VaR, CVaR, Kelly sizing, concentration alerts
Deliverable: Traders can write factors, backtest, and construct portfolios
Multi-gate execution framework with regime/risk/microstructure checks
Paper trading mode (simulated orders) with execution analytics
Immutable audit trail with LLM-generated explanations (Agents 2, 4)
Deliverable: Full-chain trading workflow with audit & compliance
Performance optimization (latency targets: p95 <100ms per module)
Scalability testing (1000 tickers, 10 concurrent users, 100k events/day)
Security audit and compliance certification (if required)
Fama–French factors (momentum, value, size, profitability, investment)
Mean reversion and reversal literature (Jegadeesh & Titman)
Sharpe Ratio, Information Coefficient, Max Drawdown (risk metrics)
Excess return above a benchmark (e.g., strategy return - SPY return)
Simulation of a strategy over historical data to evaluate performance
How well a model's confidence matches realized accuracy
One bar on a candlestick chart (OHLCV: open, high, low, close, volume)
Conditional Value at Risk; average loss in worst-case scenarios
Domain-Specific Language; simplified language for expressing factors
Expected Calibration Error; measure of confidence calibration
Computed feature that predicts stock returns (e.g., momentum, volatility)
Execution of an order at a market price
Execution check (regime, risk, microstructure) that must pass before order is sent
Hidden Markov Model; probabilistic model of market regimes
Information Coefficient; correlation between factor and future returns
IC / Std(IC); information ratio of factor
Formula for optimal position sizing given edge and odds
Time delay from input to output (e.g., signal to execution)
Large Language Model; AI for text generation (GPT-4, etc.)
Sharpe Ratio; risk-adjusted return metric (annual return / annual vol)
Difference between intended and realized fill price
Portfolio churn rate (rebalancing frequency and magnitude)
Value at Risk (95%); potential loss in worst 5% of days
Standard deviation of returns; measure of risk
Standardized value: (x - mean) / std; units of standard deviations
